title: Functions, Callbacks, Context, and Memory Interfaces · Failure Case: Allocator Injection
summary: A plugin registry invokes callbacks after their contexts change ownership and one implementation uses an incompatible signature. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem allocator injection increment in typed callback and context registry. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject matched allocate, resize, and release operations with context and failure semantics.
Bind callback, context pointer, ownership, thread, and unregister lifetime into one interface contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Allocator Injection · Theory Allocator Injection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 9526. Produce bounded C evidence to bind callback results to explicit context lifetime; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
